attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker who exploits fatigue in breakaway scenarios on final climbs, capable of winning from reduced groups rather than solo long-range efforts.
team_tactics: Limited observable team support; appears to operate independently within breakaways, suggesting either diminished team backing or tactical flexibility to hunt stage wins outside GC support.
terrain_preference: Mountainous stages with final climb finishes where he can attack from pre-formed breakaway groups; thrives when selective racing has already reduced competition.
vulnerability: Unclear GC standing suggests vulnerability in sustained stage racing hierarchy; may struggle when team prioritizes other leaders, forcing opportunistic rather than supported racing.
key_patterns: Attacks from breakaways on final climbs rather than initiating moves from GC group.
Capitalizes on fatigue of breakaway companions in closing kilometers to secure victories.
Operates with apparent autonomy, suggesting secondary status within team GC hierarchy.
form_trajectory: positive: Stage victory at Vuelta indicates returning form after reported demotion, showing ability to convert opportunities despite reduced team support
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
